title: coleus kassneri
intro: Coleus kassneri, also known as Coleus pumilus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Coleus kassneri include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antioxidant activity, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Coleus kassneri can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the leaves in a tincture or capsule form, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
